seoTitle: Split a Transaction in QuickBooks Online Correctly
metaDescription: Learn how to split a single transaction across multiple categories in QuickBooks Online for accurate expense tracking and reporting.
intro: A single payment sometimes needs to be divided across multiple expense categories or projects, and entering it as one lump sum hides that detail from your reports. This guide shows how to split a transaction in QuickBooks Online so each portion is categorized correctly. It's useful for small business owners, freelancers, and accountants managing mixed-purpose transactions.

steps.heading: Locate the transaction to split
steps.body: Find the specific transaction in your bank feed or register that needs to be divided across categories.

steps.heading: Open the split option
steps.body: Select the split transaction feature to begin dividing the total amount into separate line items.

steps.heading: Assign categories and amounts
steps.body: Enter each portion of the transaction with its correct category or class, making sure the amounts add up to the total.

steps.heading: Save the split transaction
steps.body: Confirm the split is accurate and save it so your records reflect the correct allocation.

steps.heading: Review reports for accuracy
steps.body: Check your expense reports afterward to confirm the split amounts are showing up correctly under each category.

faq.question: Can I split a transaction across more than two categories?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online lets you divide a transaction into as many line items as needed, as long as the total matches.

faq.question: Why would I need to split a transaction instead of entering it once?
faq.answer: Splitting is useful when a single payment covers multiple expense types, like a bill that includes both supplies and equipment.

faq.question: Does splitting a transaction affect bank reconciliation?
faq.answer: No, as long as the split total matches the original transaction amount, reconciliation with your bank statement stays accurate.

faq.question: Can I edit a split transaction after saving it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can go back and adjust the categories or amounts in a previously split transaction if needed.
